title: phlox divaricata var. canadensis
intro: Phlox divaricata var. canadensis, commonly known as Woodland Phlox or Eastern Blue Phlox, is a perennial flowering plant native to eastern North America. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, particularly in the treatment of wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The medicinal properties of Phlox divaricata var. canadensis are attributed to its high concentration of antioxidants and flavonoids, which have been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in poultices and salves to treat skin conditions and injuries.
